name: Solo Developer
description: Memory structure for individual developers working on personal or solo projects. Covers your role, coding preferences, and project context.

memories.name: User Role
memories.description: Who you are, your technical background, and what you typically build
content_prompts: What is your name and primary role (e.g., full-stack dev, backend engineer, mobile dev)?
What languages and frameworks do you use most often?
What kind of projects do you typically work on (web apps, CLI tools, libraries, etc.)?
What is your experience level and any specializations?
memories.example_content:
---
type: user
name: User Role
description: Background and technical identity for a solo developer
---

- Name: Jane Smith, full-stack developer
- Primary stack: TypeScript, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL
- Typically builds SaaS products and developer tools
- 8 years experience, specializes in performance optimization

memories.name: Coding Preferences
memories.description: How you like code written — style, patterns, and conventions you follow
content_prompts: Do you prefer functional or object-oriented patterns? Any specific design patterns you favor?
What are your naming conventions (camelCase, snake_case, etc.) and file organization preferences?
How do you handle errors — throw exceptions, return Result types, error codes?
What testing approach do you prefer (TDD, write tests after, specific frameworks)?
Any code style rules that matter to you (line length, imports ordering, comments style)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: feedback
name: Coding Preferences
description: Code style, patterns, and conventions for consistent AI-generated code
---

- Prefer functional patterns with immutable data where practical
- Use camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for types/classes
- Error handling: prefer Result<T, E> pattern over thrown exceptions
- Always write unit tests; use vitest for TS projects
- Keep functions under 30 lines; extract helpers aggressively

memories.name: Current Project
memories.description: What you are actively building — goals, architecture, and current status
content_prompts: What is the project name and its one-line purpose?
What is the tech stack and high-level architecture?
What are the current priorities or milestones you are working toward?
Are there any known technical debt items or constraints to be aware of?
memories.example_content:
---
type: project
name: Current Project
description: Active project context including goals, stack, and current priorities
---

- Project: TaskFlow — a CLI task management tool
- Stack: Rust + clap for CLI, SQLite for storage
- Current milestone: v0.3 release with recurring tasks
- Tech debt: migration system needs refactor before adding more schema changes
